PLANNED: the location-scale-scale model (Ayumi, issue #3). NOT a grammar ambiguity (`sd_phylo()` is defined to target the mu endpoint) and NOT a gate: with q_phylo=2 the C++ multiplies the field_effect of EVERY endpoint k by the mu per-group SD surface (src/drmTMB.cpp:928-933), which would silently fit a wrong model where the sigma phylo SD is forced equal to the mu per-species SD. The R parameter map already frees the non-target endpoint's log_sd_phylo (R/drmTMB.R:15412-15416). Needs a per-endpoint scaling rule in C++. ATTEMPT 1 (2026-07-08) implemented exactly that and FAILED its recovery gate: with tau>0 the surface inverts (beta_s (0.83,-0.06) vs truth (-0.70,0.60)) while conv=0 and pdHess=TRUE. Reverted. Leading suspect: the surface scales only tip rows while the GMRF prior is over the AUGMENTED node set, so it is not a similarity transform of the field. See docs/design/222 Attempt 1.
